name: naoqi_driver
description: Driver module between Aldebaran's NAOqiOS and ROS2. It publishes all sensor and actuator data as well as basic diagnostic for battery, temperature. It subscribes also to RVIZ simple goal and cmd_vel for teleop.
maintainers: Victor Paléologue, Marine Chamoux, Maxime Busy, Surya Ambrose, Karsten Knese
readme: naoqi_driver2 repo defines naoqi_driver package ROS2. driver charge providing bridging capabilities ROS2 NAOqiOS. works naoqi_driver ROS node. connects robot running NAOqi libQi. support audio features, naoqi_driver opens public endpoint, random port default. avoid opening public endpoint, disable audio features setting audio argument false boot_config.json, set qi_listen_url listen local endpoint, qi_listen_url:=tcp://127.0.0.1:12345. Installation Dependencies run, driver requires naoqi_libqi, naoqi_libqicore naoqi_bridge_msgs packages. installed apt-get released ROS distro) source. Additionally, pepper_meshes and/or nao_meshes display robot RViz. Ubuntu, install sudo apt-get install ros-<distro>-naoqi-libqi ros-<distro>-naoqi-libqicore ros-<distro>-naoqi-bridge-msgs ros-<distro>-pepper-meshes ros-<distro>-nao-meshes Installing source ROS2 workspace, clone repo dependencies: <ws>/src git clone https://github.com/ros-naoqi/naoqi_driver2.git vcs import naoqi_driver2/dependencies.repos rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro <distro> install vcs: sudo apt-get install python3-vcstool build workspace: colcon build --symlink-install Meshes built platforms. skip building arguments: colcon build --packages-skip nao_meshes pepper_meshes License meshes source repositories include pepper_meshes2 nao_meshes2, require interactive agreement agree license terms BY-NC-ND pepper_meshes2 LICENSE, nao_meshes2 LICENSE). skip interactive prompt setting AGREE_TO_NAO_MESHES_LICENSE I_AGREE_TO_PEPPER_MESHES_LICENSE environment variables: I_AGREE_TO_NAO_MESHES_LICENSE=1 I_AGREE_TO_PEPPER_MESHES_LICENSE=1 colcon build --symlink-install Automated jobs non-interactive environments (DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive) defaults agreeing licenses, assuming author job agreed license terms. Launch Avoid interference autonomous life full control robot ROS, disable autonomous behaviors ssh nao@<robot_host> qicli call ALAutonomousLife.setState disabled qicli call ALMotion.wakeUp NAOqi driver launched remote machine source /opt/ros/<distro>/setup.bash source <ws>/install/setup.bash built source ros2 launch naoqi_driver naoqi_driver.launch.py nao_ip:=<robot_host> qi_listen_url:=tcp://0.0.0.0:0 NAOqi higher Username password arguments required robots running NAOqi greater: ros2 launch naoqi_driver naoqi_driver.launch.py nao_ip:=<robot_host> nao_username:=nao nao_password:=<robot_password> qi_listen_url:=tcp://0.0.0.0:0 Docker container run naoqi_driver Docker container audio features enabled, libQi endpoint port source /opt/ros/<distro>/setup.bash source <ws>/install/setup.bash built source ros2 launch naoqi_driver naoqi_driver.launch.py nao_ip:=<robot_host> qi_listen_url:=tcp://0.0.0.0:56000 expose container. robot machine run driver robot machine running virtual robot), omit options: ros2 launch naoqi_driver naoqi_driver.launch.py Check node running correctly Check driver connected: ros2 node info /naoqi_driver world robot ros2 topic pub /speech std_msgs/String "data: Listen words setup speech recognition result. ros2 action send_goal listen naoqi_bridge_msgs/action/Listen "expected: language: Move head Check move head publishing /joint_angles: ros2 topic pub /joint_angles naoqi_bridge_msgs/JointAnglesWithSpeed "{header: {stamp: frame_id: joint_names: ['HeadYaw', 'HeadPitch'], joint_angles: speed: relative: published message ros2 topic echo /joint_angles Move Check move robot publishing cmd_vel robot move: ros2 topic pub /cmd_vel geometry_msgs/Twist "linear: angular: room robot! stop robot, send message linear angular velocities set ros2 topic pub /cmd_vel geometry_msgs/Twist "linear: angular: Development Check install driver source, docker compose reproducible dev environment: ROS_DISTRO=iron docker compose dev --build logs live build debuggable binaries, build command: colcon build --event-handlers console_direct+ --symlink-install --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug works running tests: colcon test --event-handlers console_direct+ --ctest-args tests Build Status ROS Distro Binary Status Source Status GitHub Status Humble Iron

name: naoqi_libqi
description: Aldebaran's libqi: a core library for NAOqiOS development
maintainers: Victor Paléologue, Maxime Busy, Surya Ambrose
readme: ROS port libQi libQi middleware RPC, type-erasure, cross-language interoperability, abstractions, logging facilities, asynchronous task management, dynamic module loading. Compilation Clone project ROS workspace src/), run colcon build. shows features framework, refer documentation details. #include <boost/make_shared.hpp> #include <qi/log.hpp> #include <qi/applicationsession.hpp> #include <qi/anyobject.hpp> qiLogCategory("myapplication"); class MyService public: void myFunction(int val) qiLogInfo() "myFunction called val; qi::Signal<int> eventTriggered; qi::Property<float> angle; register service type-system QI_REGISTER_OBJECT(MyService, myFunction, eventTriggered, angle); void print() qiLogInfo() "print called"; int main(int argc, char* argv[]) qi::ApplicationSession app(argc, argv); connect session included app app.start(); qi::SessionPtr session app.session(); register service session->registerService("MyService", boost::make_shared<MyService>()); service middleware qi::AnyObject obj session->service("MyService").value(); call myFunction obj.call<void>("myFunction", call print seconds qi::async(&print, qi::Seconds(2)); block ctrl-c app.run(); run program ./myservice --qi-standalone standalone server ./myservice --qi-url tcp://somemachine:9559 connect galaxy sessions Links Upstream repository: http://github.com/aldebaran/libqi Documentation: http://doc.aldebaran.com/libqi/ IRC Channel: freenode. Upstream Maintainers: Joël Lamotte jlamotte@aldebaran.com Jérémy Monnon jmonnon@aldebaran.com Matthieu Paindavoine matthieu.paindavoine@softbankrobotics.com Vincent Palancher vincent.palancher@external.softbankrobotics.com package.xml ROS maintainers. name: nav2_bringup
description: Bringup scripts and configurations for the navigation2 stack
maintainers: Michael Jeronimo, Steve Macenski, Carlos Orduno
readme: nav2_bringup nav2_bringup package bringup system Navigation2 applications. Pre-requisites: Install ROS Install Navigation2 ```sudo apt install ros--navigation2 Install Navigation2 Bringup ```sudo apt install ros-<ros2_distro>-nav2-bringup Install robot specific package (ex:Turtlebot Launch Navigation2 Simulation Gazebo Pre-requisites: Install Gazebo gazebo_ros_pkgs ROS2 installed system ```sudo apt-get install ros--gazebo* Gazebo world simulating robot ([Gazebo tutorials](http://gazebosim.org/tutorials?tut=quick_start)) map world saved map.pgm map.yaml ([ROS Navigation Tutorials](https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation2/tree/master/doc/use_cases)) Terminal Launch Gazebo [turtlebot3_gazebo models](https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/turtlebot3_simulations/tree/ros2/turtlebot3_gazebo/models) details ```bash export GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH=$GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH:<full/path/to/my_robot/models> gazebo --verbose libgazebo_ros_init.so <full/path/to/my_gazebo.world> Terminal Launch robot specific transforms turtlebot3_gazebo details source /opt/ros/dashing/setup.bash export TURTLEBOT3_MODEL=waffle ros2 launch turtlebot3_bringup turtlebot3_state_publisher.launch.py use_sim_time:=True Terminal Launch Navigation2 source /opt/ros/dashing/setup.bash ros2 launch nav2_bringup bringup_launch.py use_sim_time:=True autostart:=True map:=<full/path/to/map.yaml> Terminal Run RViz Navigation2 config file source /opt/ros/dashing/setup.bash ros2 run rviz2 rviz2 $(ros2 pkg prefix nav2_bringup)/share/nav2_bringup/launch/nav2_default_view.rviz RViz: map Localize robot Pose Estimate” button. transforms odom present. (odom->base_link->base_scan) Send robot goal “Navigation2 Goal” button. Note: ROS2 Action send goal, pop-up window screen ‘cancel’ button cancel view robot model RViz: Add “RobotModel”, set “Description Source” “File”, set “Description File” urdf file robot turtlebot3_burger.urdf)” Advanced: single-terminal launch convenience file launch Gazebo, RVIZ Navigation2 single command: ros2 launch nav2_bringup tb3_simulation_launch.py <settings> <settings> replace default options, world:=<full/path/to/gazebo.world> map:=<full/path/to/map.yaml> rviz_config_file:=<full/path/to/rviz_config.rviz> simulator:=<gzserver gazebo> bt_xml_file:=<full/path/to/bt_tree.xml> running command sourcing ROS2 workspace, setting path Gazebo model defining robot model source <full/path/to/ros2/setup.bash> export GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH=$GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH:<full/path/to/my_robot/models> export TURTLEBOT3_MODEL=waffle file launching robots independent navigation stacks ros2 launch nav2_bringup multi_tb3_simulation_launch.py <settings> Launch Navigation2 Robot Pre-requisites: Run SLAM Navigation tele-op drive robot generate map area testing directions assume map area. Learn Navigation SLAM create maps; Navigation SLAM note nav2_bringup works provide map file. providing map required Navigation2. Navigation2 configured costmaps navigate area map file Publish transforms robot base_link base_scan Terminal Launch Navigation2 map.yaml source /opt/ros/dashing/setup.bash ros2 launch nav2_bringup bringup_launch.py map:=<full/path/to/map.yaml> map_type:=occupancy Terminal Launch RVIZ source /opt/ros/dashing/setup.bash ros2 run rviz2 rviz2 $(ros2 pkg prefix nav2_bringup)/share/nav2_bringup/launch/nav2_default_view.rviz RVIZ: transforms odom present. name: nav2_costmap_2d
description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
    data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
    on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
    2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
    This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
    costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
    and configuration of sensor topics.

name: nav2_voxel_grid
description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.

name: dynamic_edt_3d
description: The dynamicEDT3D library implements an inrementally updatable Euclidean distance transform (EDT) in 3D. It comes with a wrapper to use the OctoMap 3D representation and hooks into the change detection of the OctoMap library to propagate changes to the EDT.

name: odom_to_tf_ros2
description: A simple ros2 package (node) that reads an odom topic and generates the equivalent tf connection (transformation). It also provides options to override frame names, or just use the ones in the original odom topic.
readme: odom_to_tf_ros2 simple ros2 package (node) reads odom topic generates equivalent connection (transformation). options override frame names, original odom topic. Parameters frame_id: parent frame override. (Leave empty odom topic) child_frame_id: child frame override. (Leave empty odom topic) odom_topic: nav_msgs/Odometry topic subscribe inverse_tf: true, generated base_link odom frame. default, generated odom frame base_link. (default: false)

name: picknik_ament_copyright
description: Check PickNik-specific copyright headers.
readme: picknik_ament_copyright Adds entrypoints ament_copyright utility checking PickNik’s headers copyright info proprietary licenses. Installation Clone build repo ROS package paths. Command Line add missing PickNik-proprietary license headers files current directory, run command (picknik sets PickNik copyright holder, picknik_proprietary closed-source proprietary license template). ament_copyright --add-missing picknik picknik_proprietary insert license header source code files license header ament_copyright recognizes: Copyright PickNik rights reserved. Unauthorized copying code base medium strictly prohibited. Proprietary confidential. pre-commit basic .pre-commit-config.yaml file automatically inserts PickNik proprietary license source code files: repos: repo: local hooks: ament_copyright ament_copyright language: system entry: ament_copyright args: ['--add-missing', 'picknik', 'picknik_proprietary',

Issues bugs affecting enhanced heuristic. fixed crashes exhibits strange behaviour, running option, small domain problem file pair passing POPF fixes bug, email Andrew Coles files debugging. doesn’t fix problem, POPF crashes, compile debugging information. description: Message definitions for reporting statistics for topics and system resources.
readme: metrics_statistics_msgs Package ROS message definitions reporting statistics topics system resources. Messages defined package StatisticDataType message represent types statistics reported. defines data types represent individual metric data points. average minimum maximum stddev sample_count StatisticDataPoint message represents single statistic includes: data_type: Type metric data point, defined StatisticDataType. data: metric data point. MetricsMessage message represents statistic data measured source time window. includes: measurement_source_name: Source measurement statistic originates. metrics_source: metric. unit: Unit representing metric. window_start: Start time metric measurement window. window_stop: time metric measurement window. statistics: list StatisticDataPoint representing values collected metrics. Install configuration: Installing: /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1.0.0 Installing: /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1 Installing: /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so Up-to-date: /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp Installing: /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/hardware.hpp Installing: /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/joint.hpp ビルドに成功すると、CMakeのデフォルトインストールディレクトリ(例：/usr/local/lib、/usr/local/include)に共有ライブラリ(librt_manipulators_cpp.so)とヘッダーファイル(rt_manipulators_cpp/*.hpp)がインストールされます。 test.cpp -lrt_manipulators_cpp uninstall_library.bash ./uninstall_library.bash /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1.0.0 /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1 /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/hardware.hpp /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/joint.hpp ライブラリの使い方はサンプル集のREADME.mdを参照してください。 hardware.hpp/cpp Hardwareクラスを実装しています hardware_communicator.hpp/cpp Hardwareクラスのうち、Dynamixelとの通信機能を実装しています hardware_joints.hpp/cpp Hardwareクラスのうち、ジョイント情報を扱う機能を実装しています joints.hpp/cpp config_file_parser.hpp/cpp dynamixel_* 各Dynamixelと通信するためのデータ変換を担います kinematics.hpp/cpp kinematics_utils.hpp/cpp link.hpp GoogleTestのインストール mkdir ~/gtest ~/gtest curl https://github.com/google/googletest/archive/release-1.11.0.tar.gz tar -xvf release-1.11.0.tar.gz mkdir googletest-release-1.11.0/build googletest-release-1.11.0/build cmake sudo install ./run_test_library.bash compiler identification GNU CXX compiler identification GNU Check working compiler: /usr/bin/cc Check working compiler: /usr/bin/cc works Detecting compiler ABI info Start JointTest.initialize_id Test JointTest.initialize_id Passed sec tests passed, tests failed Total Test time (real) sec

name: sbg_driver
description: ROS driver package for communication with the SBG navigation systems.
readme: sbg_driver ROS2 driver package SBG Systems IMU, AHRS INS. driver package sbgECom binary protocol read data configure SBG Systems devices. Initial work ENSTA Bretagne. Author: SBG Systems Maintainer: SBG Systems Contact: support@sbg-systems.com Features driver supports features: Configure ELLIPSE products yaml files note Parse IMU/AHRS/INS/GNSS sbgECom protocol Publish standard ROS messages detailed specific SBG Systems topics Subscribe RTCM data support DGPS/RTK mode centimeters-level accuracy Calibrate magnetic field on-board ELLIPSE algorithms [!NOTE] ELLIPSE devices configured ROS driver. High Performance INS EKINOX, APOGEE QUANTA, sbgInsRestApi Installation Installation Packages User install sbg_ros2_driver standard ROS installation system. Galactic sudo apt-get install ros-galactic-sbg-driver Foxy sudo apt-get install ros-foxy-sbg-driver Building sources Dependencies Robot Operating System (ROS) sbgECom Library (embeds v4.0.1987-stable compatible ELLIPSE firmware Building Clone repository Release version) Build ROS colcon build system colcon_ws/src git clone https://github.com/SBG-Systems/sbg_ros2_driver.git sbg_ros2_driver rosdep update rosdep install --from-path colcon build source install/setup.bash Usage run default Ros2 node default configuration ros2 launch sbg_driver sbg_device_launch.py run magnetic calibration node ros2 launch sbg_driver sbg_device_mag_calibration_launch.py Config files Default config files configuration file defined structure. sbg_device_uart_default.yaml config file default UART connection device. configure device ROS node, previously configured (manually ROS node). defines outputs device: /sbg/imu_data, /sbg/ekf_quat ROS standard outputs /imu/data, /imu/velocity, /imu/temp /sbg/status, /sbg/utc_time /imu/utc_ref sbg_device_udp_default.yaml config file default Udp connection device. configure device ROS node, previously configured (manually ROS node). defines outputs device: /sbg/imu_data, /sbg/ekf_quat ROS standard outputs /imu/data, /imu/velocity, /imu/temp /sbg/status, /sbg/utc_time /imu/utc_ref config files ellipse_A_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-A. ellipse_E_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-E external NMEA GNSS. ellipse_N_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-N internal GNSS. ellipse_D_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-D internal GNSS. Launch files Default launch files sbg_device_launch.py Launch sbg_device node handle received data, load sbg_device_uart_default.yaml configuration. sbg_device_mag_calibration_launch.py Launch sbg_device_mag node calibrate magnetometers, load ellipse_E_default.yaml configuration. Nodes sbg_device node sbg_device node handles communication connected device, publishes SBG output Ros environment subscribes topics RTCM data streams. Published Topics SBG Systems specific topics SBG Systems defined proprietary ROS messages report detailed information AHRS/INS. messages match sbgECom logs output device. /sbg/status sbg_driver/SbgStatus information general status (Communication, Aiding, /sbg/utc_time sbg_driver/SbgUtcTime UTC time reference. /sbg/imu_data sbg_driver/SbgImuData IMU status, sensors values. /sbg/ekf_euler sbg_driver/SbgEkfEuler Computed orientation Euler angles. /sbg/ekf_quat sbg_driver/SbgEkfQuat Computed orientation Quaternion. /sbg/ekf_nav sbg_driver/SbgEkfNav Computed navigation data. /sbg/ekf_vel_body sbg_driver/SbgEkfVelBody Computed velocity expressed INS body/vehicle frame. /sbg/ekf_rot_accel_body sbg_driver/SbgEkfRotAccel Computed rotations rate accelerations INS body/vehicle frame. /sbg/ekf_rot_accel_ned sbg_driver/SbgEkfRotAccel Computed rotations rate accelerations North, East, (NED) navigation frame. /sbg/mag sbg_driver/SbgMag Calibrated magnetic field measurement. /sbg/mag_calib sbg_driver/SbgMagCalib Magnetometer calibration data. /sbg/ship_motion sbg_driver/SbgShipMotion Heave, surge sway data. /sbg/gps_vel sbg_driver/SbgGpsVel GPS velocities GPS receiver. /sbg/gps_pos sbg_driver/SbgGpsPos GPS positions GPS receiver. /sbg/gps_hdt sbg_driver/SbgGpsHdt GPS true heading dual antenna system. /sbg/gps_raw sbg_driver/SbgGpsRaw GPS raw data post processing. /sbg/odo_vel sbg_driver/SbgOdoVel Odometer velocity. /sbg/event[ABCDE] sbg_driver/SbgEvent Event sync pin. /sbg/pressure sbg_driver/SbgPressure Pressure data. ROS standard topics order define ROS standard topics, requires SBG messages, merged. ROS standard, activate needed SBG outputs. /imu/data sensor_msgs/Imu IMU data. Requires /sbg/imu_data /sbg/ekf_quat. /imu/temp sensor_msgs/Temperature IMU temperature data. Requires /sbg/imu_data. /imu/velocity geometry_msgs/TwistStamped IMU velocity data. Requires /sbg/imu_data. /imu/mag sensor_msgs/MagneticField IMU magnetic field. Requires /sbg/mag. /imu/pres sensor_msgs/FluidPressure IMU pressure data. Requires /sbg/pressure. /imu/pos_ecef geometry_msgs/PointStamped Earth-Centered Earth-Fixed position. Requires /sbg/ekf_nav. /imu/utc_ref sensor_msgs/TimeReference UTC time reference. Requires /sbg/utc_time. /imu/nav_sat_fix sensor_msgs/NavSatFix Navigation satellite fix Global Navigation Satellite System. Requires /sbg/gps_pos. /imu/odometry nav_msgs/Odometry UTM projected position relative valid INS position. Requires /sbg/imu_data /sbg/ekv_nav /sbg/ekf_euler /sbg/ekf_quat. Disabled default, set odometry.enable configuration file. NMEA topics driver publish NMEA GGA messages internal GNSS receiver. party NTRIP client modules support VRS networks providers. Namespace ntrip_client topic_name nmea customized .yaml config files. Subscribed Topics RTCM topics sbg_device node subscribe RTCM topics published party ROS2 modules. Incoming RTCM data forwarded INS internal GNSS receiver enable DGPS/RTK solutions. Namespace ntrip_client topic_name rtcm customized .yaml config files. sbg_device_mag node sbg_device_mag node execute board in-situ magnetic field calibration. planning magnetic based heading, mandatory perform magnetic field calibration clean magnetic environnement. ELLIPSE products support magnetic based heading feature on-board magnetic field calibration process. Services /sbg/mag_calibration std_srvs/Trigger Service start/stop magnetic calibration. /sbg/mag_calibration_save std_srvs/Trigger Service save FLASH memory latest computed magnetic field calibration. HowTo Configure SBG device SBG Ros driver user configure device starting data parsing. set parameter config file. Configuration device ROS. confWithRos: true modify desired parameters config file, Firmware Reference Manual, features configurable, parameter values Configure RTK/DGPS sbg_device node subscribe rtcm_msgs/Message topics differential corrections INS internal GNSS receiver. Calibrate magnetometers ELLIPSE products magnetometers determine heading. calibration required compensate soft hard iron distortions due vehicle product installed magnetic calibration procedure held clean magnetic environnement buildings). read information magnetic field calibration procedure SBG Systems Support Center. ROS driver dedicated node easily ELLIPSE board magnetic field calibration algorithms. Start magnetic calibration session ready map magnetic field: ros2 launch sbg_driver sbg_device_mag_calibration_launch.py ros2 service call /sbg/mag_calibration std_srvs/srv/Trigger response: std_srvs.srv.Trigger_Response(success=True, message=’Magnetometer calibration process started.’) Rotate unit map surrounding magnetic field (ideally, perform axis pointing downward). covered orientations, compute magnetic field calibration: ros2 service call /sbg/mag_calibration std_srvs/srv/Trigger response: std_srvs.srv.Trigger_Response(success=True, message=’Magnetometer calibration finished. output console calibration information.’) happy results (Quality, Confidence), apply save magnetic calibration parameters. continue rotate product perform computation repeat step ros2 service call /sbg/mag_calibration_save std_srvs/srv/Trigger response: std_srvs.srv.Trigger_Response(success=True, message=’Magnetometer calibration uploaded device.’) Reset/Power Cycle device accurate magnetic based heading. name: system_modes
description:
The system modes concept assumes that a robotics system is built
    from components with a lifecycle. It adds a notion of (sub-)systems,
    hiararchically grouping these nodes, as well as a notion of modes
    that determine the configuration of these nodes and (sub-)systems in
    terms of their parameter values.

name: clober_navigation
description: clober navigation packages
readme: Clober Navigation Install Nav2 pakcage sudo apt-get install ros-foxy-nav2* Install Groot Install BehaviorTree.CPP Package original source https://github.com/BehaviorTree/BehaviorTree.CPP sudo apt-get install libzmq3-dev libboost-dev Install Groot ROS2 original source https://github.com/BehaviorTree/Groot mkdir groot_ws/src groot_ws/src git clone https://github.com/BehaviorTree/Groot.git colcon build --symlink-install Run Clober (Simulation) Launch Simulation World export GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH=${GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH}:~/clober_ws/src/clober/clober_simulation/models source /opt/ros/foxy/setup.bash source ~/clober_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 launch clober_simulation clober_world.launch.py Launch Navigation source /opt/ros/foxy/setup.bash source ~/clober_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 launch clober_navigation bringup.launch.py Set Initialpose Click Pose Estimate”, set estimation approximate location robot map. Set Goal Click Goal Pose”, set goal free space map. Waypoint Mode Start Waypoint Mode, click “Waypoint mode” bottom left. Click “Navigation2 Goal”, set waypoint goals free space map. click “Start Navigation” bottom left. Launch Groot groot_ws/build/groot ./Groot Start Monitor Select “Monitor”, click “START”. Click “Connect” left side Groot, Behavior Tree! Tuning Guide Set Navigation2 Parameters Navigation2 stack parameters change performances. improve navigation’s performances, tune basic parameters /param/clober_params.yaml. information Navigation2’s parameters Navigation2 Configuration Guide. min_x_veloticy_threshold parameter sets linear velocity odometry values threshold(m/s). min_theta_velocity_threshold parameter sets angular velocity odometry values threshold(rad/s). min_vel_x parameter sets minimum linear velocity(m/s). max_vel_x parameter sets maximum linear velocity(m/s). max_vel_theta parameter sets maximum angular velocity(rad/s). acc_lim_x parameter sets maximum linear acceleration(m/s^2). acc_lim_theta parameter sets maximum angular acceleration(rad/s^2). sim_time parameter sets time simulate by(s). parameter pass narrow area, high, rotate rapidly. critics List critic plugins critic plugins decide controller’s behavior critic’s parameters. information critics Navigation2 Configuration Guide DWB Controller’s Trajectory Critics required_movement_radius parameter check navgation progress. parameter sets minimum amount robot move progressing goal(m). movement_time_allowance parameter check navgation progress parameter set maximum amount time robot move minimum radius(s). xy_goal_tolerance parameter check goal state. parameter sets tolerance meet goal completion criteria(m). yaw_goal_tolerance parameter check goal state. parameter sets tolerance meet goal completion criteria(rad). resolution parameter sets resolution pixel costmap(m). robot_radius parameter sets robot’s radius. parameter smaller robot’s radius, occurs collision, larger, occurs inefficient path planning. inflation_radius parameter sets radius inflate costmap lethal obstacles. parameter set larger robot_radius Set Navigation2 Behavior Tree Behavior Tree Navigator module implements NavigateToPos task interface. based Behavior Tree Navigation2 provies plugins navigation. BehaviorTree.xml file, customize Navigator based information Navigation2’s plugins Navigation2 Configuration Guide Behavior Tree XML Nodes

CLPE-G-Series multi camera grabber board GMSL camera. drivers exposes camera images information CLPE-G-Series ROS messages image_transport framework. branch ROS driver. ROS driver, switch noetic branch. Environment(PC Specification) Model: Nuvo-8208GC CPU Cores CPU Clock 3.7Ghz DRAM Size DRAM Type DDR4 System Requirements Requirements: CANLAB CLPE-G-Series supplied running Ubuntu CLPE_G_Series_SDK ROS Foxy ROS Galactic Note: strongly recommended install Ubuntu shipped CLPE-G-Series. ROS binaries installed debian packages. running Ubuntu ROS built source officially supported Ubuntu Alternatively, containers CLPE-G-Series drivers properly passed Setup System dependencies sudo apt update sudo apt install git cmake wget gcc libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-dev python3-colcon* python3-rosdep python3-vcstool ROS Installation Depending version Ubuntu installed, follow instructions links install ROS binaries source. source ROS source /opt/ros/foxy/setup.bash binaries installed source ~/ros2_foxy/install/setup.bash installed source link Note: Replace foxy galactic ROS Galactic Install CLPE SDK ROS Driver Create workspace mkdir ~/ws_clpe2/src ~/ws_clpe2/ wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/canlab-co/clpe_ros/main/clpe.repos vcs import src clpe.repos Install dependencies ~/ws_clpe2 source ROS workspace command sudo rosdep init rosdep update rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro $ROS_DISTRO Build driver ~/ws_clpe2 source ROS workspace colcon build --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release build benchmarking scripts colcon build --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCLPE_ROS_BUILD_BENCHMARKS=On Run driver DDS Configuration image transport performance DDS, recommend FastDDS Shared Memory Transport enabled. copy fastdds.xml config file suitable directory, $HOME/fastdds.xml ~/ws_clpe2/src canlab-co/clpe_ros/fastdds.xml add lines ~/.bashrc export RMW_IMPLEMENTATION=rmw_fastrtps_cpp export FASTRTPS_DEFAULT_PROFILES_FILE=$HOME/fastdds.xml source ~/.bashrc terminals launching ROS nodes including driver. Launch ~/ws_clpe2 source install/setup.bash UYVY ros2 launch clpe_ros clpe_ros.launch.py password:=<sudo-password> slave:=n encoding:=yuv422 timestamp:=xavier JPEG ros2 launch clpe_ros clpe_ros.launch.py password:=<sudo-password> slave:=n encoding:=jpeg timestamp:=local Note: sudo_password number, pass password:=\'42\' equipped slave CLPE-G-Series, enter slave:=n default driver publish topics camera /clpe/cam_X/image_raw: raw image published sensor_msgs::msg::Image. /clpe/cam_X/compressed: JPEG image published sensor_msgs::msg::CompressedImage. default encoding yuv422. supported encodings, Configuration Frame rate sensor CLCC-G-01X UYVY fps JPEG fps CLCC-G-02X UYVY fps JPEG fps ROS2 Component driver supported ROS2 component. run start component container ros2 run rclcpp_components component_container terminal, load component ros2 component load /ComponentManager clpe_ros clpe::ClpeComponentNode password:=<password> Check component loaded ros2 component list Configuration driver supports ROS parameters configure behavior. password, slave, encoding timestamp parameters overwritten run time. parameters modified launch file. Note: encoding “jpeg”, timestamp set “local”. Key Description Default password sudo password *Required* slave check slave, supported arguments Defaults encoding Image encoding, supported formats bgr8, bgra8, rgb8, rgba8, mono16, yuv422, jpeg. Defaults yuv422. Note encodings yuv422 jpeg incurs conversion overhead. yuv422 timestamp Image timestamp, supported formats xavier, local. Defaults xavier. xavier cam_{n}_enable Enable camera true cam_{n}_pose Pose relative base, values roll, pitch, yaw] cam_{n}_frame_id Defines frame_id static transformations refers base_link cam_{n}_image_qos Sets QoS topic published. values strings: SYSTEM_DEFAULT, PARAMETER_EVENTS, SERVICES_DEFAULT, PARAMETERS, DEFAULT, SENSOR_DATA, HID_DEFAULT DEFAULT depth EXTRINSICS_DEFAULT DEFAULT depth transient local durabilty). SENSOR_DATA cam_{n}_info_qos Sets QoS topic published. values strings: SYSTEM_DEFAULT, PARAMETER_EVENTS, SERVICES_DEFAULT, PARAMETERS, DEFAULT, SENSOR_DATA, HID_DEFAULT DEFAULT depth EXTRINSICS_DEFAULT DEFAULT depth transient local durabilty). name: crane_plus_control
description: CRANE+ V2 control package
readme: crane_plus_control このパッケージはros2_control をベースにした、CRANE+ ros2_control関連ファイル crane_plus_control::CranePlusHardware (crane_plus_hardware) 本パッケージがエクスポートするHardware Componentsです CRANE+ crane_plus_description/urdf/crane_plus.ros2_control.xacroから読み込まれます launch/crane_plus_control.launch.py Controller Managerとコントローラを起動するlaunchファイルです config/crane_plus_controllers.yaml Controller Managerのパラメータファイルです crane_plus_hardwareがCRANE+ PCとCRANE+ 正しく設定できていない場合、CRANE+ USB通信ポートの設定 crane_plus_hardwareはUSB通信ポート（/dev/ttyUSB*）を経由してCRANE+ /dev/ttyUSB0を使用する場合 sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 latency_timerの設定 CRANE+ USB通信ポートとサーボモータの設定を変更します。 下記のコマンドを実行してUSB通信ポートのlatency_timerを変更します。 参考資料：https://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_wizard2/#usb-latency-setting /dev/ttyUSB0を使用する場合 rootに切り替える sudo echo /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer exit Return Delay Timeの設定 CRANE+ V2に搭載されているサーボモータDynamixel AX-12A にはReturn Delay Timeというパラメータがあります。 サーボモータがInstruction Packetを受信してからStatus Packetを送信するまでに500 usecの遅れがあります。 Dynamixel Wizard を使用してRetrun Delay Timeを小さくすると、制御周期が早くなります。 crane_plus_control.launch.pyを実行すると、Controller Managerノードが起動し、 crane_plus_joint_state_controller (joint_state_controller/JointStateController) crane_plus_arm_controller (joint_trajectory_controller/JointTrajectoryController) crane_plus_gripper_controller (joint_trajectory_controller/JointTrajectoryController) 次のコマンドでジョイント角度情報（joint_states）を表示できます ros2 topic echo /joint_states Controller Managerのパラメータ Controller Managerのパラメータは config/crane_plus_controllers.yaml controller_manager: ros__parameters: update_rate: crane_plus_arm_controller: type: joint_trajectory_controller/JointTrajectoryController crane_plus_gripper_controller: type: joint_trajectory_controller/JointTrajectoryController joint_state_controller: type: joint_state_controller/JointStateController update_rateは制御周期を設定します。 CRANE+ CRANE+ V2の腕の制御用にcrane_plus_arm_controllerを、 グリッパの制御用にcrane_plus_gripper_controllerを設定しています。 crane_plus_hardwareのパラメータ crane_plus_hardwareのパラメータは crane_plus_description/urdf/crane_plus.urdf.xacro <xacro:arg name="use_gazebo" default="false" <xacro:arg name="port_name" default="/dev/ttyUSB0" <xacro:arg name="baudrate" default="1000000" <xacro:arg name="timeout_seconds" default="5.0" <xacro:arg name="read_velocities" default="0" <xacro:arg name="read_loads" default="0" <xacro:arg name="read_voltages" default="0" <xacro:arg name="read_temperatures" default="0" USB通信ポート port_nameはCRANE+ V2との通信に使用するUSB通信ポートを設定します。 baudrateはCRANE+ V2に搭載したDynamixelとの通信ボーレートを設定します。 デフォルト値にはDynamixel AX-12Aの最高ボーレートである1000000 Mbps)を設定しています。 timeout_secondsは通信タイムアウト時間（秒）を設定します。 crane_plus_hardwareは、一定時間（デフォルト5秒間）通信に失敗し続けると、 read/write動作を停止します。 USBケーブルや電源ケーブルが抜けた場合等に有効です。 read_velocities、read_loads、read_voltages、read_temperatures 読み取ったパラメータはdynamic_joint_statesトピックとしてパブリッシュされます。 ros2 topic echo /dynamic_joint_states top

name: crane_plus_description
description: CRANE+ V2 description package
readme: crane_plus_description このパッケージはCRANE+ V2のモデルデータ(xacro)を所持しています。 display robot model 下記のコマンドを実行して、robot_state_publisher、joint_state_publisher、rviz2を起動します。 CRANE+ V2のモデルが表示されるので、xacroファイルのデバッグに役立ちます。 ros2 launch crane_plus_description display.launch.py Webカメラ搭載モデルの場合は、下記のコマンドを実行してください。 ros2 launch crane_plus_description display.launch.py use_camera:=true configure servo angle limits CRANE+ Angle Limit、CCW Angle Limit）を設定してください。 CRANE+ V2に搭載されているサーボモータはROBOTISのAX-12Aのため、 Dynamixel Wizard crane_plus.urdf.xacroには、 <xacro:property name="M_PI" <xacro:property name="TO_RADIAN" value="${M_PI <xacro:property name="SERVO_HOME" value="${TO_RADIAN <xacro:property name="JOINT_VELOCITY_LIMIT" <xacro:property name="JOINT_1_LOWER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_1_UPPER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_2_LOWER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_2_UPPER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_3_LOWER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_3_UPPER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_4_LOWER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_4_UPPER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_HAND_LOWER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/> <xacro:property name="JOINT_HAND_UPPER_LIMIT" TO_RADIAN SERVO_HOME}"/>

name: crane_plus_examples
description: CRANE+ V2 examples package
readme: crane_plus_examples このパッケージはCRANE+ ROS CRANE+ V2本体をPCに接続する CRANE+ V2本体をPCに接続します。 ※CRANE+ USB通信ポートの接続を確認する USB通信ポートの設定についてはcrane_plus_controlの README 正しく設定できていない場合、CRANE+ move_groupとcontrollerを起動する 標準のCRANE+ 次のコマンドでmove_group (crane_plus_moveit_config)と controller (crane_plus_control)を起動します。 ros2 launch crane_plus_examples demo.launch.py port_name:=/dev/ttyUSB0 Webカメラ搭載モデルを使用する場合 Webカメラ搭載モデルの場合は、次のコマンドを実行してください。 ```は使用するWebカメラを指定してください。 ros2 launch crane_plus_examples demo.launch.py port_name:=/dev/ttyUSB0 use_camera:=true video_device:=/dev/video0 準備（Ignition Gazeboを使う場合） move_groupとIgnition Gazeboを起動する 次のコマンドでmove_group (crane_plus_moveit_config)と Ignition Gazeboを起動します。 ros2 launch crane_plus_ignition crane_plus_ignition.launch.py ros2 launch crane_plus_examples example.launch.py example:='gripper_control' 終了するときはCtrl+cを入力します。 Ignition Gazeboでサンプルプログラムを実行する場合 Ignition Gazeboでサンプルプログラムを実行する場合はuse_sim_timeオプションを付けます。 ros2 launch crane_plus_examples example.launch.py example:='gripper_control' use_sim_time:='true' Examples demo.launch.pyを実行している状態で各サンプルを実行できます。 gripper_control pose_groupstate joint_values pick_and_place 実行できるサンプルの一覧は、examples.launch.pyにオプション-sを付けて実行することで表示できます。 ros2 launch crane_plus_examples example.launch.py Arguments (pass arguments ':='): 'example': Set executable [gripper_control, pose_groupstate, joint_values, pick_and_place] (default: 'gripper_control') gripper_control ros2 launch crane_plus_examples example.launch.py example:='gripper_control' :='): 'example': Set executable [color_detection] (default: 'color_detection') aruco_detection モノに取り付けたArUcoマーカをカメラで検出し、マーカ位置に合わせて掴むコード例です。 マーカはaruco_markers.pdfをA4紙に印刷して、一辺50mmの立方体に取り付けて使用します。 tfのframe_idはマーカIDごとに異なりID0のマーカのframe_idはtarget_0になります。 掴む対象はtarget_0に設定されています。 マーカ検出にはOpenCVを使用しています。 ros2 launch crane_plus_examples camera_example.launch.py example:='aruco_detection' Videos list color_detection tfのframe_idはtarget_0です。 色検出にはOpenCVを使用しています。 ros2 launch crane_plus_examples camera_example.launch.py example:='color_detection' Videos list

name: crane_plus_gazebo
description: CRANE+ V2 gazebo simulation package
readme: crane_plus_gazebo CRANE+ のGazeboシミュレーションパッケージです。 crane_plus_ignition 次のコマンドを実行するとGazeboが起動し、CRANE+ V2モデルとTableが表示されます。 初回起動時はTableのモデルをダウンロードするため、モデルの表示に時間がかかることがあります。 実機との接続やcrane_plus_examples/launch/demo.launch/pyの実行は必要ありません。 ros2 launch crane_plus_gazebo crane_plus_gazebo.launch.py

name: crane_plus_ignition
description: CRANE+ V2 ignition gazebo simulation package
readme: crane_plus_ignition CRANE+ のIgnition Gazebo 次のコマンドを実行するとIgnition Gazeboが起動し、CRANE+ V2モデルとTable、Boxが表示されます。 初回起動時はTableとBoxのモデルをダウンロードするため、モデルの表示に時間がかかることがあります。 実機との接続やcrane_plus_examples/launch/demo.launch/pyの実行は必要ありません。 ros2 launch crane_plus_ignition crane_plus_ignition.launch.py

name: crane_plus_moveit_config
description: CRANE+ V2 move_group config package
readme: crane_plus_moveit_config このパッケージはCRANE+ V2のmove_group設定ファイル及びlaunchファイルを含んでいます。 ros-planning/moveit2/moveit_demo_nodes/run_move_group run_move_group.launch.pyを実行すると、move_groupやrviz等のノードが起動します。 CRANE+ V2本体を動かすことはできません。(crane_plus_examplesを参照してください。) configファイル controllers.yaml moveit_simple_controller_managerのパラメータを設定しています 設定内容はcrane_plus_controlのコントローラ名やコントローラタイプに依存します crane_plus.srdf move_groupとしてarm、gripperを設定しています armのgourp_stateとしてhome、verticalを設定しています kinematics.yaml armのkinematics_solverを設定しています デフォルトのKDLKinematicsPluginでは軌道計画に失敗するため、LMAKinematicsPluginを使用しています ompl_planning.yaml Open Motion Planning Libraryのパラメータを設定しています ros-planning/moveit_resources/panda_moveit_configのパラメータを流用しています

name: grepros
description: grep for ROS bag files and live topics: read, filter, export
readme: grepros grep ROS bag files live topics: read, filter, export. Searches ROS messages matches message field regular expression patterns plain text, field type. specific values specific message fields default, matches printed console. Additionally, matches written bagfile HTML/CSV/MCAP/Parquet/Postgres/SQL/SQLite, published live topics. Supports ROS1 ROS2. ROS environment variables set, ROS_VERSION. Supported bag formats: .bag (ROS1), (ROS2), .mcap (ROS1, ROS2). ROS1, messages grepped Python packages message types installed. ROS1 live topics requires ROS master running. ROS2 requires Python packages message types path. Supports loading custom plugins, additional output formats. Usable Python library, LIBRARY.md. Full API documentation https://suurjaak.github.io/grepros. usage Installation Inputs Outputs Matching filtering Plugins Notes ROS1 ROS2 command-line arguments Dependencies Attribution License usage Search text” bags current directory subdirectories: grepros text" Print lines message live ROS topic: grepros --max-per-topic --lines-per-message --live Find message “future” (case-sensitive) my.bag: grepros future --max-count my.bag Find messages, geometry_msgs package, “map” frame, bags current directory, reindexing unindexed bags: grepros frame_id=map --type geometry_msgs/* --max-count --reindex-if-unindexed Pipe diagnostics messages “CPU usage” live ROS topics my.bag: grepros "CPU usage" --type *DiagnosticArray --no-console-output --write my.bag Find messages field “key” topics “diagnostics”, bags “/tmp”: grepros key=0xA002 --topic *diagnostics --path /tmp Find diagnostics_msgs messages bags current directory, “navigation” fields “message”, print header stamp values: grepros --type diagnostic_msgs/* --select-field message --emit-field header.stamp status.values navigation Print message lidar topic ROS1 host highlight: ROS_MASTER_URI=http://1.2.3.4::11311 grepros --live --topic *lidar* --max-per-topic --no-highlight Export bag messages SQLite Postgres, print export progress: grepros my.bag --write my.bag.sqlite --no-console-output --no-verbose --progress grepros my.bag --write postgresql://user@host/dbname --no-console-output --no-verbose --progress Patterns Python regular expression syntax, message matches match. wildcards simple globbing characters, target matches matches. Note expressions quoted avoid shell auto-unescaping auto-expanding linear.x=2.?5 "linear.x=2\.?5". Care unquoted wildcards, auto-expanded shell happen match paths disk. Installation grepros written Python, supporting Python Python Developed tested ROS1 Noetic ROS2 Foxy, works ROS2 versions (Galactic, Humble, Iron, Rolling); works earlier ROS1 version Melodic (lacking MCAP Parquet support). pip pip install grepros add grepros command path. Requires ROS Python packages (ROS1: rospy, roslib, rosbag, genpy; ROS2: rclpy, rosidl_parser, rosidl_runtime_py, builtin_interfaces). ROS1, bag files live topics, minimal ROS1 Python packages installed separately pip install rospy rosbag roslib roslz4 --extra-index-url https://rospypi.github.io/simple/ apt ROS apt repository added system: sudo apt install ros-noetic-grepros ROS1 Noetic sudo apt install ros-foxy-grepros ROS2 Foxy add grepros command global ROS1 ROS2 environment. catkin ROS1 workspace, source directory: git clone https://github.com/suurjaak/grepros.git grepros catkin build add grepros command local ROS1 workspace path. colcon ROS2 workspace, workspace root: git clone https://github.com/suurjaak/grepros.git src/grepros colcon build --packages-select grepros add grepros command local ROS2 workspace path. name: kobuki_ftdi
description:
Utilities for flashing and enabling Kobuki's USB connection.
	    This package contains tools for flashing the Kobuki's FTDI chip (usually done at the factory).
	    The special firmware for the FTDI chip (USB to serial converter) enables it to appear as
	    /dev/kobuki on the user's PC.
readme: Kobuki FTDI Works][Is Working?][Flashing Device] package tools factory-flashing FTDI usb-to-serial device kobuki troubleshooting dependency running kobuki. Works Kobuki’s FTDI chip flashed special identifier programs uniquely identify device kobuki. turn udev rules conveniently establish presence *nix filesystem /dev/kobuki. Source Installation Ubuntu Prerequisites sudo apt install python3-rosdep python3-colcon-common-extensions Build Create colcon workspace don’t mkdir colcon_ws/src Clone repository src folder colcon_ws/src git clone https://github.com/maliput/maliput.git Install package dependencies rosdep export ROS_DISTRO=foxy rosdep update rosdep install --rosdistro $ROS_DISTRO --from-paths src Build package colcon build --packages-up-to maliput **Note**: build documentation `-BUILD_DOCS` cmake flag required: colcon build --packages-select maliput --cmake-args -DBUILD_DOCS=On" info refer Source Installation Ubuntu development recommended follow guidelines setting development workspace Profiling maliput maliput able run profiler evaluating performance. implemented ign-common3’s profiler component. order avoid performance drop maliput dependency chain clean, disabled default binaries aren’t distributed profiler enabled. profiler enabled maliput, mandatory install maliput source. Steps Install prerequisites. ign-common3 installed rosdep profiler run demand: sudo apt install libignition-common3-profiler-dev Build maliput package MALIPUT_PROFILER_ENABLE cmake argument: Continue Source-Installation-on-Ubuntu instructions. difference colcon build --packages-select maliput --cmake-args -DMALIPUT_PROFILER_ENABLE=On" Run application preferred maliput backend. terminal, open visualizer profiler: ign_remotery_vis _Note: opens browser `xdg-open`, recommended installed `xdg-utils` browser: `sudo apt install xdg-utils firefox`)_. name: openzen_driver
description: ROS 2 Driver for LP-Research inertial measurement units and satellite navigation senors
readme: OpenZen Node ROS software sensor data sensor connected OpenZen ROS version OpenZen library high performance sensor data streaming processing supports multiple sensor models: https://bitbucket.org/lpresearch/openzen/ Requirements Serial Port Access Rights call, logout login user ensure changed permissions effect. access sensors connected USB, ensure user running ROS sensor node access /dev/ttyUSB devices. adding user dialout group. sudo adduser <username> dialout Compilation compile driver ROS2 setup, follow steps: mkdir ros_ws/src ros_ws/src git clone --recurse-submodules https://bitbucket.org/lpresearch/openzenros2.git ROS2 environment source /opt/ros/foxy/setup.bash colcon build source ./install/setup.bash Running Driver run OpenZen ROS2 driver command window compile software: ros2 run openzen_driver openzen_node --ros-args --remap __ns:=/openzen default, connect sensor. connect specific sensor, serial sensor parameter, ros2 run openzen_driver openzen_node --ros-args --remap __ns:=/openzen sensor_name:="LPMSCU2000573" sensor configured baud rate, baudrate parameter give specfic baud rate setting: ros2 run openzen_driver openzen_node --ros-args --remap __ns:=/openzen sensor_name:="LPMSCU2000573" baudrate:=115200 print IMU values ROS ros2 topic echo /openzen/data output values GPS unit command: ros2 topic echo /openzen/nav plot values linear acceleration) ros2 run rqt_plot rqt_plot /openzen/data/angular_velocity prepared sample launch file openzen_lpms.launch demonstrate data acquisition plotting openzen_sensor_node: ros2 launch openzen_driver openzen_lpms.launch.py change autocalibration setting command line: ros2 service call /enable_gyro_autocalibration std_srvs/SetBool "{data: true}" trigger gyroscope calibration: ros2 service call /calibrate_gyroscope std_srvs/Trigger

name: plansys2_msgs
description: Messages and service files for the ROS2 Planning System
readme: PlanSys2 Messages Representing PDDL Expressions Trees PDDL expressions conveniently represented trees. PDDL expression. (robot_at millennium_falcon)(not (robot_at death_star))) root node tree corresponds outer parentheses encompassing statement. statement children. child corresponds predicate (robot_at millennium_falcon) child corresponds expression (robot_at death_star)). Similarly, expression child predicate (robot_at death_star). tree node implemented simple class object form class Node public String public Node[] children; node data relevant node links children. tree stored list nodes. Representing PDDL Expressions ROS Messages PlanSys2 requires PDDL construct trees passed nodes system. define PDDL tree ROS, custom messages, plansys2_msgs/Tree plansys2_msgs/Node. contents plansys2_msgs/Tree simply array type plansys2_msgs/Node. plansys2_msgs/Node[] nodes contents plansys2_msgs/Node data relevant node links children. uint8 node_type uint8 expression_type uint8 modifier_type uint32 node_id uint32[] children string plansys2_msgs/Param[] parameters float64 bool negate node_type defines type PDDL construct node represents. values supported PlanSys2. uint8 uint8 uint8 uint8 ACTION uint8 PREDICATE uint8 FUNCTION uint8 EXPRESSION uint8 FUNCTION_MODIFIER uint8 NUMBER node_type EXPRESSION, expr_type field define type expression represented. expressions types supported PlanSys2. uint8 COMP_GE uint8 COMP_GT uint8 COMP_LE uint8 COMP_LT uint8 ARITH_MULT uint8 ARITH_DIV uint8 ARITH_ADD uint8 ARITH_SUB node_type FUNCTION_MODIFIER modifier_type field define type function modifier represented. function modifier types supported PlanSys2. uint8 ASSIGN uint8 INCREASE uint8 DECREASE uint8 SCALE_UP uint8 SCALE_DOWN node_id corresponds node’s location nodes list takes range len(nodes)-1]. children list specifies locations, node id’s, node’s children. string parameters list predicate function nodes, defined list parameters. field PDDL functions stores numeric Finally, negate field tracks inverse truth node parent PREDICATE node, PREDICATE node negate node’s Actions plansys2_msgs::action::ExecutePlan start continuously monitor execution plan. Messages plansys2_msgs::msg::Action PDDL action, preconditions effects defined plansys2_msgs::msg::Tree. plansys2_msgs::msg::ActionExecution establish communication protocol action executors (implemented behavior trees) action executor clients (user lifecyle nodes perform actions). plansys2_msgs::msg::ActionExecutionInfo provide feedback execution status action execution client. plansys2_msgs::msg::ActionPerformerStatus provide status feedback action executor client executor. plansys2_msgs::msg::DurativeAction PDDL durative action, requirements effects defined plansys2_msgs::msg::Tree. plansys2_msgs::msg::Knowledge message internally system, feedback user knowledge item added, removed, modified. plansys2_msgs::msg::Node base node message creating PDDL construct trees. discussion details. plansys2_msgs::msg::Param define PDDL parameters instances. plansys2_msgs::msg::Tree Consists list plansys2_msgs::msg::Node defines PDDL construct tree. discussion details. Services plansys2_msgs::srv::AddProblemGoal add goal represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Tree problem expert. plansys2_msgs::srv::AffectNode add, remove, modify predicate function represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Node. plansys2_msgs::srv::AffectParam add remove problem instance represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Param plansys2_msgs::srv::ClearProblemKnowledge clear problem instances, predicates, functions. plansys2_msgs::srv::ExistNode check existence predicate function represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Node. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetDomain Returns domain string. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetDomainActionDetails action optional list parameters, returns action (plansys2_msgs::msg::Action). optional parameter list empty define returned action parameters. auto-generated values optional parameter list retrieve instantiated plan action. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetDomainActions Returns list domain action names. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetDomainDurativeActionDetails durative action optional list parameters, returns durative action (plansys2_msgs::msg::Action). optional parameter list empty define returned durative action parameters. auto-generated values optional parameter list retrieve instantiated plan action. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetDomainTypes Returns domain types. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetNodeDetails Returns predicate function node represented (plansys2_msgs::msg::Node). domain expert client, node problem expert client, full construct string plansys2_msgs::srv::GetOrderedSubGoals Returns ordered list sub-goals sub-goal represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Tree. sub-goals ordered start times plan. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetPlan Returns generated plan list times, actions (represented strings), durations. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetProblem Returns problem string. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetProblemGoal Returns entire problem goal represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Tree. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetProblemInstanceDetails parameter returns instance represented (plansys2_msgs::msg::Param). plansys2_msgs::srv::GetProblemInstances Returns list problem instances instance represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Param. plansys2_msgs::srv::GetStates return list predicates functions predicate function defined plansys2_msgs::msg::Node. plansys2_msgs::srv::IsProblemGoalSatisfied Specifies goal, represented plansys2_msgs::msg::Tree, satisfied. plansys2_msgs::srv::RemoveProblemGoal Removes problem goal, including sub-goals.

name: massrobotics_amr_sender
description: MassRobotics AMR Interop Sender
readme: massrobotics_amr_sender Configuration-based ROS2 package sending MassRobotics AMR Interop Standard messages compliant receivers. Package installation binary packages node released package added manually ROS2 build installation running command: sudo apt-get install ros-foxy-massrobotics-amr-sender Alternatively, add package rosdep dependency install running rosdep update Building source ros2 installed properly. clone repository src folder local workspace build package executing commands: Create ROS2 workspace mkdir ~/ros2_ws/src ros2_ws/ Clone repo workspace git clone https://github.com/inorbit-ai/ros_amr_interop.git ./src Install dependencies rosdep update rosdep install --ignore-src --from-paths src/ Run build colcon build --packages-select massrobotics_amr_sender Node configuration configuration file define ROS2 messages mapped AMR Interop Standard messages. sample_config.yaml reference. Running sender node node takes MassRobotics AMR config file path parameter. assumed file current directory. Remember source ROS2 environment binary installation workspace overlay source install/setup.bash Launch node pointing configuration file ros2 launch massrobotics_amr_sender massrobotics_amr_sender.launch.py config_file:=/path/to/config.yaml Running tests local workspace: colcon test --packages-select massrobotics_amr_sender colcon test-result --verbose

name: ros_ign_bridge
description: Bridge communication between ROS and Ignition Transport
readme: Bridge communication ROS Ignition Transport package network bridge enables exchange messages ROS Ignition Transport. bridge implemented point support service calls. support limited message types: ROS type Ignition Transport type std_msgs/msg/Bool ignition::msgs::Boolean std_msgs/msg/Empty ignition::msgs::Empty std_msgs/msg/Float32 ignition::msgs::Float std_msgs/msg/Float64 ignition::msgs::Double std_msgs/msg/Header ignition::msgs::Header std_msgs/msg/Int32 ignition::msgs::Int32 std_msgs/msg/String ignition::msgs::StringMsg geometry_msgs/msg/Quaternion ignition::msgs::Quaternion geometry_msgs/msg/Vector3 ignition::msgs::Vector3d geometry_msgs/msg/Point ignition::msgs::Vector3d geometry_msgs/msg/Pose ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/msg/PoseStamped ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/msg/Transform ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/msg/TransformStamped ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/msg/Twist ignition::msgs::Twist mav_msgs/msg/Actuators (TODO) ignition::msgs::Actuators (TODO) nav_msgs/msg/Odometry ignition::msgs::Odometry rosgraph_msgs/msg/Clock ignition::msgs::Clock sensor_msgs/msg/BatteryState ignition::msgs::BatteryState sensor_msgs/msg/CameraInfo ignition::msgs::CameraInfo sensor_msgs/msg/FluidPressure ignition::msgs::FluidPressure sensor_msgs/msg/Imu ignition::msgs::IMU sensor_msgs/msg/Image ignition::msgs::Image sensor_msgs/msg/JointState ignition::msgs::Model sensor_msgs/msg/LaserScan ignition::msgs::LaserScan sensor_msgs/msg/MagneticField ignition::msgs::Magnetometer sensor_msgs/msg/PointCloud2 ignition::msgs::PointCloudPacked tf2_msgs/msg/TFMessage ignition::msgs::Pose_V trajectory_msgs/msg/JointTrajectory ignition::msgs::JointTrajectory Run ros2 run ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge instructions. Ignition Transport talker ROS listener Start parameter bridge watch topics. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 run ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge /chatter@std_msgs/msg/String@ignition.msgs.StringMsg start ROS listener. Shell /opt/ros/foxy/setup.bash ros2 topic echo /chatter start Ignition Transport talker. Shell ign topic pub /chatter ignition.msgs.StringMsg 'data:"Hello"' ROS talker Ignition Transport listener Start parameter bridge watch topics. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 run ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge /chatter@std_msgs/msg/String@ignition.msgs.StringMsg start Ignition Transport listener. Shell ign topic /chatter start ROS talker. Shell /opt/ros/foxy/setup.bash ros2 topic pub /chatter std_msgs/msg/String "data: 'Hi'" Run bridge exchange images generate Ignition Transport images Ignition Gazebo, converted ROS images, visualized rqt_image_viewer. start Ignition Gazebo. Shell ign gazebo sensors_demo.sdf Let’s topic camera images published. Shell ign topic grep image /rgbd_camera/depth_image /rgbd_camera/image start parameter bridge previous topic. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 run ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge /rgbd_camera/image@sensor_msgs/msg/Image@ignition.msgs.Image start ROS GUI: Shell /opt/ros/foxy/setup.bash ros2 run rqt_image_view rqt_image_view /rgbd_camera/image current images rqt_image_view coming Gazebo (published Ignition Msgs Ignition Transport). screenshot shows shell windows expected content ROS Foxy Ignition Edifice): Static bridge run executable starts bidirectional bridge specific topic message type. static_bridge executable installed bridge. example’s code ros_ign_bridge/src/static_bridge.cpp. code, bridge hardcoded bridge string messages published /chatter topic. Let’s give starting Ignition ROS terminal start bridge: ros2 run ros_ign_bridge static_bridge terminal start ROS listener: ros2 topic echo /chatter std_msgs/msg/String terminal publish Ignition message: ign topic pub /chatter ignition.msgs.StringMsg 'data:"Hello"' point, ROS listener echoing message. let’s ROS Ignition. terminal start Igntion listener: ign topic /chatter terminal publish ROS message: ros2 topic pub /chatter std_msgs/msg/String 'data: "Hello"' Ignition listener echoing message.

name: system_metrics_collector
description: Node and aggregation utilities to measure and publish system metrics.
readme: System metrics collector Supported targets system metrics collector package supports ROS Distro ROS eloquent Ubuntu Bionic Description package aims integrate lightweight aggregation tools order collect, measure, publish aggregate metrics. moving average tools provide constant time sample aggregation produce average, min, max, standard deviation, sample count. collector thread-safe mechanism perform measurements. PeriodicMeasurementNode ROS2 Lifecycle Node utilizes aggregation tools abstraction ROS2: specifically configurable timer (default perform measurements, published (default minute configurable) topic (default /system_metrics). lifecycle state transitions automatically invoked Node activated state creation. goal start metric collection automatically launching. lifecycle actions (activate, deactivate, shutdown) manually invoked. Parameters parameters defined: measurement_period: Parameter measurement_period Type: integer Description: period milliseconds measurement Constraints: Read true Min Max Step: publish_period: Parameter publish_period Type: integer Description: period milliseconds published MetricsMessage. measurement_period. Constraints: Read true Min Max Step: Usage: provide multiple entry points package examples directory, demonstrate run metric collection nodes instrument existing nodes order measure performance. ROS2 Launch talker_listener_example launch file demonstrates measure CPU memory ROS2 process. Specifically, instruments demo_nodes_cpp talker listener nodes launches separate processes, system CPU memory measurement nodes. run ros2launch. ros2 launch system_metrics_collector talker_listener_example.launch.py system_cpu_and_memory_configuration_example launched ros2launch, demonstrates configure metric collection nodes. ros2 launch system_metrics_collector system_cpu_and_memory.launch.py Manual Execution run example_main executable manually start system CPU, system memory, process CPU process memory measurement nodes. sets nodes’ verbosity levels DEBUG, print collection status information console. ros2run ros2 run system_metrics_collector example_main Change publish_period measurement_period --ros-args: ros2 run system_metrics_collector example_main --ros-args measurement_period:=100 -publish_period node generate nodes. ros2node ros2 node list /linuxCpuCollector /linuxMemoryCollector /linuxProcessCpuCollector /linuxProcessMemoryCollector Interaction ROS2 CLI section describes interact package ROS2 Command Line Interface (CLI). output relevant. Subscribe /system_metrics visualize statistics. ros2topic ros2 topic echo /system_metrics measurement_source_name: linuxMemoryCollector metrics_source: system_memory_percent_used unit: percent window_start: sec: nanosec: window_stop: sec: nanosec: statistics: data_type: data: data_type: data: data_type: data: data_type: data: data_type: data: measurement_source_name: linuxCpuCollector metrics_source: system_cpu_percent_used unit: percent window_start: sec: nanosec: window_stop: sec: nanosec: statistics: data_type: data: data_type: data: data_type: data: data_type: data: data_type: data: Review parameters ros2param ros2 param list /linuxCpuCollector: measurement_period publish_period use_sim_time /linuxMemoryCollector: measurement_period publish_period use_sim_time /linuxProcessCpuCollector: measurement_period publish_period use_sim_time /linuxProcessMemoryCollector: measurement_period publish_period use_sim_time Inspect change lifecycle state ros2lifecycle List lifecycle nodes: ros2 lifecycle nodes /linuxCpuCollector /linuxMemoryCollector state specific node: ros2 lifecycle /linuxCpuCollector active Activate node (start measurement collection data publishing): ros2 lifecycle set /linuxCpuCollector activate Transitioning successful Deactivate node (stop measurement collection data publishing): ros2 lifecycle set /linuxCpuCollector deactivate Transitioning successful
